name: django
summary: The web framework for perfectionists with deadlines — batteries included

story.problem: Building a full-stack web app from scratch means wiring together a router, ORM, auth system, admin interface, form handling, CSRF protection, migrations, and templating engine — dozens of decisions and thousands of lines before you write business logic.
story.mental_model: Django follows MTV (Model-Template-View): Models describe your data and Django generates the SQL; Templates are HTML files with a simple tag language; Views are functions or classes that receive a request and return a response. The admin panel, auth system, and migration engine come free. Convention over configuration — there's a right way to do everything.
story.when_to_use: Full-stack web apps, content management systems, apps that need user auth and admin UI out of the box, large teams where consistency matters, or any project where you want decisions made for you.
story.when_not_to_use: Pure JSON APIs without a frontend — FastAPI is lighter and faster. Tiny microservices where Django's size is overhead. Real-time async apps — though Django has async views, it's not designed around it.

alternatives.name: fastapi
alternatives.reason: Async, type-hint driven — better for API-only backends

alternatives.name: flask
alternatives.reason: Lighter, more flexible — better when you want to choose your own components

alternatives.name: wagtail
alternatives.reason: CMS built on Django — for content-heavy sites

nodes.label: Model
nodes.summary: Define a database table. Field types map to columns; Django generates migrations.

nodes.label: Views (FBV / CBV)
nodes.summary: A function or class that takes an HttpRequest and returns an HttpResponse.

nodes.label: urls.py
nodes.summary: URL routing config. Maps URL patterns to views using path() or re_path().

nodes.label: QuerySet
nodes.summary: Lazy database query result. Chain .filter(), .exclude(), .order_by() before evaluating.

nodes.label: Admin site
nodes.summary: Auto-generated admin interface at /admin/. Register models to CRUD them instantly.

nodes.label: Form / ModelForm
nodes.summary: Declarative HTML forms with validation. ModelForm generates from a Model automatically.

nodes.label: Middleware
nodes.summary: Hooks that process every request/response: auth, CSRF, sessions, security headers.

nodes.label: settings.py
nodes.summary: Central config: INSTALLED_APPS, DATABASES, MIDDLEWARE, TEMPLATES, SECRET_KEY.

tasks.title: Define a model and run migrations

steps.label: Define model
steps.code:
# blog/models.py
from django.db import models

class Post(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    body = models.TextField()
    published = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title

steps.label: Migrate
steps.code:
python manage.py makemigrations
python manage.py migrate

tasks.title: Write a view and wire a URL

steps.label: View function
URL config
steps.code:
# blog/urls.py
from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('posts/', views.post_list),
]

steps.label: Include in project
steps.code:
# mysite/urls.py
from django.urls import path, include

urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('api/', include('blog.urls')),
]

steps.label: Register
steps.code:
# blog/admin.py
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Post

@admin.register(Post)
class PostAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):
    list_display = ['title', 'published', 'created_at']
    list_filter = ['published']
    search_fields = ['title', 'body']

steps.label: Login
steps.code:
# Visit http://127.0.0.1:8000/admin/
# Full CRUD UI for Post — no extra code needed
